currentUse: Former community/nonprofit center (Southwest Housing Solutions' Vista Partnership); listed for sale September 2024 at $825,000 as an 11,282 sq ft office/specialty building; outcome of the listing unverified
summary: The Šv. Antano (St. Anthony's Lithuanian) church building at the corner of West Vernor Highway and 25th Street in Southwest Detroit (addressed both as 1750 25th St and 3553 W Vernor Hwy) is still standing and was never demolished. After the parish's 2013 closure it was redeveloped by Southwest Housing Solutions' Vista Partnership as a community center for fitness, recreation, and meetings, with the organization itself based in the church; religious items and pews were removed, the ground floor hosted a charity teaching free English classes to recent immigrants, and the premises were rented for events and exercise classes. Southwest Solutions merged with Development Centers in 2023 to form MiSide. In September 2024 the building was put on the market through O'Connor Real Estate at an asking price of $825,000, marketed as an 11,282 sq ft "office/specialty" property in an opportunity zone a few blocks from Ford's Michigan Central Station, featuring "an impressive, light-flooded event hall with original hardwood floor, chandeliers and arched windows," private offices, former living spaces, and a 16-car lot. Whether that sale has closed could not be verified; listing pages remained live on aggregators as of July 2026. The Archdiocese of Detroit's official closed-parishes register confirms the parish operated 1920-2013 with records held at Divine Providence Parish (Lithuanian), Southfield. The building sits within the West Vernor-Junction Historic District, listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2002.

developments.headline: Church redeveloped as community center by Southwest Housing Solutions' Vista Partnership
developments.detail: Vista Partnership's first project was the former St. Anthony's Church at Vernor & 25th Street, redeveloped in 2013 as a community center for fitness, recreational use, and corporate meetings; Southwest Housing Solutions was based in the church. Religious items and pews were removed; the ground floor hosted a charitable institution teaching recent immigrants English for free, with the premises used for exercise classes and rented for special events. The same nonprofit also rehabilitated the adjacent Lithuanian Hall, and district building occupancy rose from roughly 30% to 80% through its redevelopment work.

sources.title: Detroit, Michigan: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

developments.headline: Southwest Solutions merges into MiSide
developments.detail: Southwest Solutions and Development Centers, the two Detroit nonprofits, merged in 2023 to form MiSide, changing the institutional owner-operator context of the former church building.

developments.headline: Former church building listed for sale at $825,000
developments.detail: O'Connor Real Estate published an offering memorandum for 3553 W Vernor Hwy (SE corner of Vernor and 25th), described as 'Formerly the home of St. Anthony's Church': approximately 11,282 sq ft, updated electrical and HVAC, event hall with original hardwood floor, chandeliers and arched windows, private offices, former living spaces, 16-car parking lot, B4 zoning, located in a qualified opportunity zone a few blocks from Ford's Michigan Central Station. Asking price $825,000. No record of a completed sale was found through July 2026.

sources.title: Detroit, Michigan: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The Archdiocese of Detroit's official closed-parishes register confirms the archive's dates exactly: 'St. Anthony (Lithuanian) Parish (Detroit) 1920-2013, records at Divine Providence Parish (Lithuanian), Southfield' — consistent with the recorded merger into Dievo Apvaizdos. Refinements: (1) Global True Lithuania states the closure decision was made by the bishop in 2011 and executed in 2013, and that after the priest's 2009 retirement only one weekly Lithuanian Sunday Mass remained, held in a ground-floor chapel because the elderly congregation could not climb to the second-floor sanctuary — texture consistent with the archive's '34 registered parishioners' decline narrative. (2) The 2024 offering memorandum and Lithuanian encyclopedia sources give the building's construction date as 1923, versus the parish founding of 1920 — a parish-vs-building distinction, not a conflict. (3) The property carries two addresses for the same corner building: 1750 25th Street (parish address) and 3553 W Vernor Hwy (commercial listing address). (4) The archive's June 23, 2013 closing date and 'Together in Faith' program name were not independently found in open web sources, but nothing contradicts them.
gaps: Could not verify: (1) the terms, date, or price of the property transfer from the Archdiocese of Detroit to Southwest Housing Solutions — no published sale record found; (2) whether the September 2024 $825,000 listing has resulted in a completed sale — LoopNet, Howard Hanna, and Real Estate One listing pages all returned HTTP 403 and no news of a closing was found through July 2026, so the building may still be on the market or may have quietly sold (MLS #20240073239 appeared in search results but the listing page could not be opened); (3) the current occupant of the building today, post-MiSide merger — whether community-center programming continues while the property is marketed; (4) whether the building is a contributing structure to the West Vernor-Junction Historic District (the district's 2002 NRHP listing is confirmed, and the church is mentioned in the district's history, but contributing status was not stated); (5) no press coverage of parishioner reunions, preservation campaigns, or Lithuanian community activity at the site since closure was found in English or Lithuanian searches. Note: Detroit has a second, unrelated 'Cathedral of St. Anthony' at 5247 Sheridan St (a former German parish, closed 2006, now an independent Ecumenical Catholic congregation) that dominates search results — care was taken not to conflate it with the Lithuanian parish at Vernor/25th.
